accomplishments: Create a theme system with CSS variables
Switch between light and dark modes
Update your entire site by changing a few values

do.title: Create your first CSS variables
steps: Create a new file and save it as css-variables.html
Copy and paste the code below
Try changing --primary-color in :root to a different color
Watch everything using that variable update instantly!
do.code:
CSS Variables Demo

What are CSS Variables?

CSS variables let you store values and reuse them throughout your stylesheet.

Why Use Them?

Change one variable, update your entire site. Perfect for themes and design systems!
do.result: You defined colors and values once, then reused them everywhere with var(--variable-name)!
do.proofRequired: Screenshot showing cards with your custom color scheme.

concepts.name: CSS Custom Properties (Variables)
concepts.definition: Store values you want to reuse throughout your stylesheet. Define with --name: value in :root, use with var(--name) anywhere. Change one value, update everything that uses it.
concepts.analogy: A nickname. Instead of writing someone's full name every time, you use their nickname. CSS variables are nicknames for values.

concepts.name: Theme switching with data attributes
concepts.definition: Define different variable values under [data-theme="dark"] or [data-theme="light"] selectors. Toggle the data-theme attribute with JavaScript to instantly switch all colors.
concepts.analogy: A light switch for your entire website — flip it and every room changes at once.

concepts.name: Design systems with variables
concepts.definition: Organize all your values (colors, spacing, typography, shadows, radii, transitions) as variables. This creates a consistent, maintainable system where everything matches.
concepts.analogy: A master control panel — adjust one dial and every part of the building responds.

understand.keyTakeaway: CSS variables make your code maintainable and your themes switchable. Define once, use everywhere, change in one place.

build.hint: Add transition: background-color 0.3s ease, color 0.3s ease to body and cards for smooth theme switching!

challenge.title: Build a Multi-Theme System
challenge.mission: Create a website with 3 different themes users can switch between, with localStorage persistence.
requirements: Three complete themes (light, dark, and one custom theme)
challenge.starterCode:
Multi-Theme Demo

Click theme buttons to switch!
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add a fourth purple/pink theme and make the active button visually highlighted!

capabilities: Create and use CSS custom properties (variables)
Build theme systems with variable scoping
Use variables for colors, spacing, typography, and effects

bridge.preview: In Lesson 4.4, you'll learn to rotate, scale, skew, and create 3D effects — taking your designs to the next level!

do.title: Transform elements
steps: Create a new file and save it as transforms.html
Copy and paste the code below
Hover over each box to see different transforms!
do.code:
CSS Transform Effects

Hover over each box to see the transform!

Rotate
Scale
Skew
Move
Multi
do.result: The transform property lets you rotate, scale, skew, and move elements — all with smooth transitions!

concepts.name: rotate(), scale(), translate()
concepts.definition: rotate(45deg) spins an element. scale(1.3) makes it 130% size. translate(50px, -20px) moves it. Can combine: transform: rotate(15deg) scale(1.2).
concepts.analogy: Giving an object physical instructions — "turn 45 degrees, grow to 130%, move up 20 pixels."​

concepts.name: 3D transforms with perspective
concepts.definition: perspective: 1000px creates 3D space. rotateY(180deg) flips a card. transform-style: preserve-3d enables 3D on children. backface-visibility: hidden hides the back.
concepts.analogy: Looking through a window at objects in 3D space — perspective controls how far away you are from the window.

concepts.name: Why transforms are special
concepts.definition: Transforms don't affect document flow (other elements don't move). They're GPU-accelerated for smooth performance. They don't trigger layout recalculation.
concepts.analogy: Moving a sticker on a whiteboard — everything underneath stays exactly where it was.

concepts.name: transform-origin
concepts.definition: Controls the pivot point for transforms. Default is center. transform-origin: top left makes rotation pivot from the corner.
concepts.analogy: The pin in a pinwheel — it determines what point the element spins around.

understand.keyTakeaway: Transform lets you rotate, scale, skew, and move elements without affecting layout. Combine with transitions for smooth interactive effects.

build.goal: Create a 3D card flip and a hover gallery with transforms
build.hint: The back card starts rotated 180deg so when the container flips, it appears facing forward!

challenge.title: Build an Animated Product Showcase
challenge.mission: Create a product card with multiple transform effects — rotation, scale, and 3D perspective.
requirements: Card rotates slightly in 3D on hover
"Add to Cart" button scales on hover
Price tag with attention-grabbing pulse animation

SALE

Create product showcases with advanced effects

bridge.preview: In Lesson 4.5, you'll learn to add shadows, blur, brightness, and other visual effects to create depth and polish!

accomplishments: Use filters to blur, brighten, and adjust images

do.title: Master box-shadow
steps: Create a new file and save it as shadows-filters.html
Copy and paste the code below
See how different shadows create different levels of depth!
do.code:
Box Shadow Examples

Subtle

Cards and containers

Medium

Interactive elements

Strong

Strong elevation

Colored

Brand personality

Layered

Multiple shadows

Inner

Inset effect
do.result: Box-shadow adds depth and elevation, making flat designs feel three-dimensional!
do.proofRequired: Screenshot showing the different shadow styles.

concepts.name: box-shadow
concepts.definition: Syntax: box-shadow: horizontal vertical blur spread color. Creates depth. Use multiple shadows (comma-separated) for realistic layered effects. Add "inset" for inner shadows.
concepts.analogy: A light source creating a shadow. Higher elevation = larger, softer shadow. Like picking up a card from a table.

concepts.name: CSS filters
concepts.definition: filter: blur(4px), grayscale(100%), brightness(150%), saturate(200%), hue-rotate(180deg). Apply visual effects to any element. Can combine: filter: blur(2px) brightness(120%).
concepts.analogy: Instagram filters for CSS — blur, black-and-white, brightness, all applied in code.

concepts.name: Glassmorphism with backdrop-filter
concepts.definition: backdrop-filter: blur(10px) blurs content BEHIND the element. Combined with rgba() semi-transparent backgrounds and subtle borders, creates the modern "frosted glass" effect.
concepts.analogy: Looking through frosted glass — you can see shapes and colors behind it, but they're blurred and softened.

understand.keyTakeaway: box-shadow creates depth, filter transforms elements visually, backdrop-filter blurs what's behind an element. Together they create modern, polished designs.

build.goal: Apply filters to images and create a glassmorphism card
build.hint: backdrop-filter needs the -webkit- prefix for Safari. Always include both!
